title: access key
text: access key A character in a widget label or menu item that when pressed, often in combination with Alt , gives focus to, or activates, the widget or menu item. Synonyms: hot key A key or combination of keys that, when pressed, gives focus to or activates a widget. Most access keys involve holding down an extra key, such as Alt , while pressing an alphabetic key, for example, Alt+E. Access keys are defined within Uniface, but are processed by the GUI, if supported. Valid alphabetic keys are indicated by mnemonics on the current form, which are highlighted or underlined, depending on the platform used. The Enter and Escape keys are also access keys. In some circumstances (for example, when choosing an item from a menu) the use of the modifier key is not required. Related Links menu A type of global object that enables users to see and execute application commands in a desktop application. A menu can be displayed as a menu bar, a drop-down menu (in a menu bar or cascaded menu), or a pop-up menu. accelerator A keystroke or combination of keystrokes that performs an action, such as Ctrl+O to open a file.
